title: Market Structure Flip Detector Tool
subtitle: ATR-Adaptive Swing Depth Validation for LH/HL Structure Flips
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — ATR-to-depth conversion formula is a direct upgrade to our StructureEngine lookback. State machine for flip detection, swing validation window. Backtested 64-83% win rate on M5/M15.

core_concept.name: Market Structure Flip
core_concept.description: Track sequential swing pivots. When in a HH sequence and a new high forms BELOW the prior high = bearish flip (LH confirmed). When in a LL sequence and a new low forms ABOVE the prior low = bullish flip (HL confirmed). ATR converts price volatility into a bar-count window for swing validation.

flip_types.bearish_flip: In uptrend (state=HH): new swing high < prior swing high = LH = bearish flip = ChoCH signal
flip_types.bullish_flip: In downtrend (state=LL): new swing low > prior swing low = HL = bullish flip = ChoCH signal

core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: This is our StructureEngine's CHOCH detection logic. Key addition: ATR-to-depth formula converts price-domain volatility into bar-count window for swing validation. Our fixed lookback (LOOKBACK_MAP) doesn't adapt to volatility — this does.

atr_to_depth_formula.formula: rawDepth = (ATR / SYMBOL_POINT) * InpAtrMultiplier; depth = max(1, floor(rawDepth * InpAtrLoosenFactor))
atr_to_depth_formula.default_result: ATR(14) * 1.0 * 0.5 / point → adaptive window in bars
atr_to_depth_formula.behavior: High volatility → wider ATR → larger depth window → requires more bars for swing validation. Low volatility → smaller window → faster flip detection.
atr_to_depth_formula.our_current: LOOKBACK_MAP fixed values (M1=2, M15=3, H1=5). NOT volatility-adaptive.
atr_to_depth_formula.upgrade: Replace LOOKBACK_MAP[tf] with atr_depth(atr, symbol_point, multiplier=1.0, loosen=0.5)

swing_detection.is_swing_high: for i in range(shift-depth, shift+depth+1): if high[i] > candidate: return False; return True
swing_detection.is_swing_low: for i in range(shift-depth, shift+depth+1): if low[i] < candidate: return False; return True
swing_detection.note: Identical to our fractal detection. The ATR-depth is the differentiator.

state_machine.bearish_flip: state==1 AND new_high < prior_high → emit SELL signal, set state=2
state_machine.bullish_flip: state==2 AND new_low > prior_low → emit BUY signal, set state=1

backtesting.note: Buy signals outperform sell signals. M5 has more signals; M15 buy rate drops (small sample).

keep: ATR-to-depth formula: rawDepth = (ATR/point)*multiplier; depth = max(1, floor(rawDepth*loosen))
State machine: HH/LL tracking with flip on first reversal
Swing validation: scan window of 'depth' bars around candidate
discard: Binary signal output (no zone, no SL/TP)
Fixed state machine (only 2 states) — our StructureEngine tracks full HH/HL/LH/LL sequence
improve: ATR-adaptive lookback in StructureEngine: replace LOOKBACK_MAP with atr_depth()
Combine flip detection with zone proximity: flip AT zone = ChoCH + zone confluence

atr_adaptive_lookback.benefit: During high volatility (e.g., news events): wider lookback catches only major swings. During calm markets: tight lookback catches micro-swings. Avoids missing HL on volatile bars.
